definition: With over 90% of leveraged loans being covenant-lite, default recognition is delayed by 12-24 months. When the credit cycle turns, this will produce step-function defaults rather than gradual rolling patterns. Current low default rates are the expected precursor to a cliff, not evidence of credit health.
evidence: Over 90% of leveraged loans are covenant-lite
Historical pattern in covenant-lite cycles: artificially low defaults then discontinuous spike
Standard credit models assume rolling defaults and do not capture step-function distribution
open_questions: The scale of the current covenant-lite cycle is unprecedented — will the step-function pattern hold at this scale?
How does covenant-lite interact with private credit opacity to mask deterioration?
